business.headline: Mukesh Ambani's Jio IPO is here: Reliance to offer up to 27 crore fresh sharesMukesh Ambani announced at Reliance's 49th AGM that Jio Platforms will file its DRHP with Sebi today. The move formally starts the long-awaited listing process and adds momentum to India's pipeline of mega public issues.

Sensex ends 600 points lower, Nifty tops 24,000; IT stocks bleedThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 607.08 points, or 0.78%, to close at 76,802.90, while the NSE Nifty50 shed 155.05 points, or 0.64%, to settle at 24,013.10.

Mukesh Ambani announces Jio IPO, draft papers to be filed with Sebi todayAddressing shareholders at Reliance Industries' 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM), Ambani said Jio Platforms' draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) has been approved by the company's board and will be filed with market regulator Sebi later today.

Government grants copyright society status to PPL India, boosting royaltiesThe government has registered PPL India as a Copyright Society under the Copyright Act, 1957. The move gives music users a clearer licensing route and is expected to improve royalty payments to rights holders and artists.

Why Infosys took the biggest hit after Accenture's warningAccenture reported strong AI demand but trimmed its growth outlook, signalling that clients are still holding back on spending. The warning hammered Indian IT stocks, with Infosys leading the rout.

Jio IPO moves ahead as Reliance bets big on AI and new energyRIL AGM 2026: Jio Platforms to file IPO papers with Sebi; Mukesh Ambani unveils five major value creation pathways, bets big on AI, energy self-reliance, satellite connectivity, media, FMCG and global expansion

Sensex down 700 points: Why is stock market falling today after a 5-day rally?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 635.85 points, or 0.84%, at 76,774.13, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 170.10 points, or 0.70%, to 23,997.90 as of 10:13 am. The decline snapped a strong rally that had seen the Sensex gain nearly 4.8% and the Nifty rise over 4.3% in the previous five sessions.

Reliance AGM: Will Mukesh Ambani finally reveal the Jio IPO date today?Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani is set to address shareholders at the company's 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM) today, and the biggest question on Dalal Street is simple: Will he finally announce a timeline for the Jio IPO?

Accenture warning sends Infosys, TCS and HCLTech tumbling. Here's whyMajor Indian IT stocks fell sharply after Accenture lowered the upper end of its annual revenue growth forecast. The sell-off has renewed concerns over weak discretionary spending, slower deal conversion and the pace of demand recovery.

Sensex, Nifty tumble as Accenture warning triggers IT crash on Dalal StreetInfosys, TCS and other technology stocks tumbled after the global IT giant flagged slower growth, dragging the broader market lower despite falling oil prices.

India's Swiss bank funds fall 8%, while direct deposits jump 50%Swiss National Bank data showed total funds linked to Indian clients fell in 2025 even as direct customer deposits rose sharply. The figures underline that Indian-linked assets in Switzerland are not the same as black money estimates and reflect a more transparent banking regime.

Jio IPO, AI, green energy: 5 big questions Mukesh Ambani may answer at RIL AGMMukesh Ambani is set to face shareholder questions on Jio's IPO timeline, AI plans and green energy projects at Reliance's 49th AGM. With key deadlines nearing, investors are seeking proof that earlier commitments are moving into execution.

Got your Form 16? Check these 6 details before filing your ITRSince Form 16 forms the foundation of income reporting for salaried taxpayers, spending a few minutes verifying the information can save you a lot of trouble later.

The third dimension – after inspectors and employers, now the consultantsIn the eleventh part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines the role of labour consultants who, instead of ensuring compliance, sometimes help employers create the appearance of compliance while facilitating violations of labour laws.

NSE IPO filing could unlock Rs 25,000 crore for SBI, Temasek and top investorsNSE has filed draft papers for its long-delayed initial public offering after nearly a decade. The move could unlock major gains for existing investors and value the exchange at about $57 billion.

8th Pay Commission: How fitment factors from 2.57 to 3.83 may change salariesEmployee unions have sought a higher fitment factor as the 8th Pay Commission considers revisions to salaries and pensions. The final multiplier will determine how much basic pay and pensions rise for millions of central government employees and retirees.

Sensex closes 254 points higher, Nifty near 24,200; IndiGo up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 254.36 points, or 0.33%, to close at 77,409.98, while the NSE Nifty50 gained around 82 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 24,168.

Crude oil is no longer the villain. A new threat could raise your household billEconomists say a weak monsoon has overtaken crude oil as India's main inflation risk. Poor rainfall could raise food prices, squeeze household budgets and complicate RBI rate cuts.

EPF accounts to get 8.25% interest soon after government approvalThe government has approved an 8.25% EPF interest rate for 2025-26, paving the way for account credits this month. The decision lets EPFO begin processing payments for over 7 crore subscribers after Finance Ministry clearance.

Why the Rs 30,000-crore NSE IPO is unlike any other IPOFor nearly 10 years, the NSE IPO was the market's longest-running "coming soon" story. Now, India's biggest exchange is finally ready to test its value in public.

entertainment.headline: How TVF cracked the code of middle-class storytelling TVF built its success by telling honest, relatable stories of middle-class India instead of chasing escapist drama. In this weeks' Cinematic Saturday TVF founder Arunabh Kumar, actors, Namita Dubey and Sunny Hinduja, and writer Nitin Tiwari talk about how TVF shows made ordinary lives feel extraordinary.

Friends director James Burrows dies at 85, cast honours sitcom legend James Burrows, Friends director and Cheers co-creator, has died at 85. The cast of the popular sitcom paid heartfelt tributes and recalled his influence on their work and lives.

Maa Inti Bangaaram box office Day 1: Samantha film registers Rs 10.7 crore globally Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaaram opened with Rs 10.70 crore worldwide on Day 1. The Telugu market drove the start, with occupancy rising sharply through the day.

Cocktail 2 box office Day 1: Shahid, Rashmika, Kriti's film records solid opening Shahid Kapoor, Rashmika Mandanna and Kriti Sanon's Cocktail 2 opened to a solid start on June 19. The film earned Rs 13.50 crore nett in India on its opening day.

Ameesha Patel says Gadar set an impossible benchmark: Not every film can be iconic Ameesha Patel reflected on Gadar's enduring pull and why audiences still return to Sakina. She said iconic films fix actors in public memory and set near-impossible expectations for later work.

Allu Arjun brings smiles to young leukaemia patient's day, recreates Pushpa scene Allu Arjun met a young leukaemia patient and recreated popular Pushpa moments with her. The widely shared interaction comes as he is set to appear in a Hyderabad court on June 22 in the Sandhya Theatre stampede case.

Anne Hathaway, 43, is pregnant with her third child Anne Hathaway has announced that she is expecting her third child with husband Adam Shulman. The social media reveal comes as she balances a packed film slate and busy family life.

Exclusive: Allu Arjun to attend court hearing on June 22 in theatre stampede case Hyderabad's Nampally Court has summoned actor Allu Arjun to appear in person in connection with the 2024 Sandhya Theatre stampede case. The actor is expected to cooperate with the legal proceedings, while his family continues to support the victims' families.

Shahid Kapoor confused by Cocktail 2 adult rating, calls it a family watch Shahid Kapoor has questioned why Cocktail 2 received an A certificate despite calling it suitable for family viewing. The issue has surfaced alongside the cast's denial of rumours about a lesbian storyline and a physical threesome.

Benny Blanco debuts half-bald look, fans react with disbelief: Feel bad for Selena Benny Blanco appeared on a YouTube podcast sporting a half-shaved haircut. The unexplained look quickly set off jokes, bald cap theories and reactions from Selena Gomez fans.

January 6 is now Diljit Dosanjh Day in Los Angeles The Los Angeles City Council has declared 6 January 2027 as Diljit Dosanjh Day. The honour recognises his role in taking Punjabi music global and expanding South Asian representation in mainstream American culture.

Meet Raakh's breakout star Ramandeep, who once played cricket with Arshdeep Singh Acor Ramandeep Yadav recalled landing Prime Video's Raakh when he was close to leaving Mumbai. The role has become his breakthrough after years of rejection and an intense journey into Rajjo's mind.

Ameesha Patel: Bollywood must question unjustified age-gap romances Actor Ameesha Patel said she initially questioned being paired opposite co-star Sunny Deol in Gadar because of their age difference. She added that age-gap romances work only when the story genuinely justifies them.

The Social Reckoning trailer: Jeremy Allen White fights to expose Mark Zuckerberg The trailer for The Social Reckoning introduces Jeremy Strong as Mark Zuckerberg in Aaron Sorkin's new film. It follows Frances Haugen and Jeff Horwitz as the drama revisits the events behind The Facebook Files.

Nooru Sami review: Director Sasi's remarriage drama means well, but trips oftenRelease Date:Fri Jun 19, 2026 Nooru Sami movie review: Director Sasi's Nooru Sami, starring Swasika, Ajay Dhishan and Vijay Antony, is a well-intentioned drama that explores remarriage. While the story great potential, it has many shortcomings in terms of its presentation.

Aamir Khan's production house announces documentary on President Droupadi Murmu Aamir Khan Productions is making a documentary on President Droupadi Murmu, directed by Svati Chakravarty Bhatkal. The film will trace her journey from an Odisha village to Rashtrapati Bhavan and examine its wider significance.

Samay Raina announces 'no filter' Latent 2, first episode to premiere on June 20 Samay Raina has confirmed that India's Got Latent 2 will stream on Netflix and YouTube on the same day. The inaugural episode of the second season will premiere on June 20.

Delhi High Court postpones Salman Khan's plea against Kala Hiran release The Delhi High Court has deferred Salman Khan's plea against the release of Kala Hiran: The Battle for Legacy to July 1. The court gave no interim relief and asked Khan to serve the full plaint on the filmmakers.

general news.headline: Be quiet or get out: Israeli envoy in shouting match with UN officialIsrael's UN envoy Danny Danon confronted senior UN officials at a public hearing over reports alleging abuses. The exchange highlighted Israel's escalating anger at UN scrutiny over the Gaza war.

All world leaders believed it: Trump reveals reason behind 'boss' remarkIn an Axios interview, Donald Trump explained his viral 'I am the boss' remark from the G7 Summit in France. He said it was a funny, light-hearted moment as the comment drew wide attention.

Monsoon rain: Clouds to begin gaining strength after weeks of deep sleepThe southwest monsoon is showing signs of revival, with rain clouds strengthening over Central India. The shift could ease rainfall deficits and support kharif sowing as thunderstorms build across the region.

India Inc is failing the country in AI and deep tech due to its dhandho mindsetAs the US moves to restrict top AI models, and the world transitions to an AI and deep tech economy, India is staring at some grave challenges. The blame, not entirely but largely, lies with India Inc and its dhandho mindset.

The Great IIT Reversal: Why are toppers choosing Civil Engineering over AI?Handcrafted ByOur Editors For years, the IIT dream meant Computer Science. But as AI reshapes careers and India's infrastructure boom gathers pace, some of the country's top JEE Advanced rankers are taking a fresh look at Civil Engineering. Their choices may signal a shift in the future of engineering careers.

Walking a fundamental right: Supreme Court wants footpaths for all roadsThe Supreme Court has declared the right to walk on safe, demarcated footpaths a fundamental right. The ruling places a binding duty on public authorities and puts pedestrians ahead of motorists' privilege.

Ultra-long-haul flights are here: Once a journey of 2 weeks will shrink to 22 hoursThe journey between Sydney and London, which once took two weeks and 40 stops, is set to shrink into a single 22-hour flight. Australian flag-carrier Qantas is preparing to launch the first direct Sydney-London flight, which will also herald the era of ultra-long-haul travel.

NEET UG re-test 2026: NTA allots Abu Dhabi centre to Nagpur studentA Nagpur student was mistakenly allotted an Abu Dhabi examination centre for the NEET UG 2026 re-exam despite selecting centres in Maharashtra. The error sparked fresh criticism of the NTA, which later acknowledged a technical glitch and promised a revised admit card.

Why was Miguel Almiron sent off vs Turkiye? FIFA's new World Cup rule explainedParaguay midfielder Miguel Almiron made unwanted FIFA World Cup history on Friday after becoming the first player to be sent off under FIFA's new mouth-covering rule during his side's Group D clash against Turkiye.

Teen drugged, raped, forced to abort pregnancy; she still wants to marry abuserA girl in Bihar's Muzaffarpur accused that a man he befriended online drugged, repeatedly sexually exploited, and forced her into an abortion under the false pretext of marriage.

Friends director James Burrows dies at 85, cast honours sitcom legendJames Burrows, Friends director and Cheers co-creator, has died at 85. The cast of the popular sitcom paid heartfelt tributes and recalled his influence on their work and lives.

Lionel Messi red card debate grows as Algeria lodge FIFA complaintFIFA World Cup 2026: Algeria have written to FIFA over refereeing decisions in their 3-0 defeat to Argentina, including a challenge by Lionel Messi on Aissa Mandi. The complaint has widened the debate over VAR and disciplinary consistency at the tournament.

Surat man fakes his own kidnapping, demands Rs 50 lakh from family. Video goes viralAn accountant from Surat staged his own kidnapping and sent ransom videos to his family, demanding Rs 50 lakh from them. Investigators believe he did it to recover heavy stock market options trading losses.

Dogs only bark, but tigers hunt: Eknath Shinde's warning to Uddhav Sena campEknath Shinde hit back at political opponents while addressing Shiv Sena workers on Friday. He said the party remains rooted among common people and is growing stronger under his leadership.

Isro's NavIC isn't alone. Indian startups are building eyes and ears in spaceAt the 2026 India Space Congress, private Indian firms unveiled satellites to observe the Earth, intercept signals and provide navigation that does not depend on foreign systems. Some of these startups say they want to strengthen Isro's NavIC, not replace it.

Sena not born to merge with anyone: Uddhav Thackeray rubbishes rebel MPs' claimThe Shiv Sena UBT chief's rebuttal came in response to six party MPs who rebelled against the leadership, saying the organisation was about to merge with the Congress.

How TVF cracked the code of middle-class storytellingTVF built its success by telling honest, relatable stories of middle-class India instead of chasing escapist drama. In this weeks' Cinematic Saturday TVF founder Arunabh Kumar, actors, Namita Dubey and Sunny Hinduja, and writer Nitin Tiwari talk about how TVF shows made ordinary lives feel extraordinary.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Driver killed, nearly 90 injured after two trains collide in north LondonVideo footage shared on social media appeared to show the front of one train tangled with the rear of another while both remained on the tracks.

Can Israel defend itself without US help? Here's the answer in yes or noAmerican Vice-President JD Vance issued a warning to Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu's Cabinet after some of his ministers said they weren't bound by the US-Iran agreement. Can Israel fight a long conventional war without support from the US? Here's the entire picture and the answer in a clear yes or no.

Yashasvi Jaiswal in focus as India eye ODI series sweep against AfghanistanIndia vs Afghanistan 3rd ODI preview: India may have already wrapped up the ODI series against Afghanistan, but all eyes will be on Yashasvi Jaiswal in the third and final match. The left-handed opener will be eager to make a statement after managing just four runs in the second ODI in Lucknow and will look to capitalise on another opportunity to post a substantial score in Chennai.

In a historic first, India gets key role in FATF as Vivek Aggarwal elected VPVivek Aggarwal's election as FATF Vice President marks a major diplomatic win for India, strengthening its influence in shaping global efforts against money laundering, terror financing and financial crime.

Neeraj Chopra breaches CWG mark, his prophecy on Pathirage comes true in Doha DLNeeraj Chopra finished fourth on his return to competition in Doha with a best throw of 85.69m. Sri Lanka's Rumesh Pathirage won with 88.68m, continuing his remarkable run of form in 2026.

Babies sold for Rs 10 lakh: Delhi Police bust trafficking racket; 13 arrestedDelhi Police has uncovered an inter-state racket that allegedly sold infants across several states. The case has exposed how forged records and vulnerable families were exploited for profit.

sports.headline: The World Cup's identity puzzle: Curaçao, Croatia and football's unique map Livano Comenencia's goal against Manuel Neuer in Curacao's World Cup match against Germany was more than a historic first for the Caribbean nation. As Curacao celebrated its maiden FIFA World Cup goal, the moment also highlighted how football allows territories and smaller nations to express a distinct identity on the global stage, turning a single goal into a symbol of pride, recognition and belonging beyond the result itself.

Why was Miguel Almiron sent off vs Turkiye? FIFA's new World Cup rule explained Paraguay midfielder Miguel Almiron made unwanted FIFA World Cup history on Friday after becoming the first player to be sent off under FIFA's new mouth-covering rule during his side's Group D clash against Turkiye.

Neymar set for Brazil return against Scotland after fitness scare Brazil coach Carlo Ancelotti has confirmed that Neymar will be available for Selecao's final Group C match against Scotland, ending uncertainty over the forward's fitness ahead of a crucial World Cup fixture.

FIFA World Cup: Brazil suffer Raphinha injury scare in Haiti win Brazil suffered an injury scare during their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C clash against Haiti after forward Raphinha was forced off in the first half at Philadelphia Stadium on Friday.

Brazil crush Haiti 3-0 as Matheus Cunha's brace sparks first win Brazil climbed to the top of Group C and kickstarted their FIFA World Cup campaign with a commanding 3-0 win over Haiti in Philadelphia on Friday, thanks to a first-half masterclass from Matheus Cunha and Vinicius Junior.

Yashasvi Jaiswal in focus as India eye ODI series sweep against Afghanistan India vs Afghanistan 3rd ODI preview: India may have already wrapped up the ODI series against Afghanistan, but all eyes will be on Yashasvi Jaiswal in the third and final match. The left-handed opener will be eager to make a statement after managing just four runs in the second ODI in Lucknow and will look to capitalise on another opportunity to post a substantial score in Chennai.

Lionel Messi red card debate grows as Algeria lodge FIFA complaint FIFA World Cup 2026: Algeria have written to FIFA over refereeing decisions in their 3-0 defeat to Argentina, including a challenge by Lionel Messi on Aissa Mandi. The complaint has widened the debate over VAR and disciplinary consistency at the tournament.

Saibari's 71-second strike sends Morocco marching on with 1-0 win vs Scotland FIFA World Cup 2026: Ismael Saibari scored after 71 seconds as Morocco beat Scotland 1-0 in Boston. The result took Morocco to four points and left Scotland needing a result against Brazil.

Shreyanka Patil and cruel cycle of injuries that keeps haunting her Women's T20 World Cup: It was only earlier this year that Shreyanka Patil made her return to the India setup after a lengthy spell on the sidelines caused by a finger injury and shin splints. Now, the 23-year-old has suffered another setback, having twisted her ankle during India's match against the Netherlands.

TUR 0 vs 1 PAR Live: Turkiye crashes out of World Cup after loss to 10-men Paraguay Turkiye (TUR) vs Paraguay (PAR) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: (TUR 0-1 PAR). Paraguay beat Turkiye to send them crashing out of the World Cup. However, it was Almiron's red card in the injury time of first half for covering his mouth during a confrontation with opponent. Earlier, Brazil cruised to a comfortable win after a dominant first-half display against Haiti in Philadelphia. Matheus Cunha starred along with Vinicius Junior to give the five-time champions three points and go top of Group C.

USA beat Australia to reach World Cup knockouts with first double in 96 years FIFA World Cup 2026: The United States beat Australia 2-0 to secure a place in the World Cup 2026 knockout stage. The win gave Mauricio Pochettino's side two victories from two matches and a first World Cup double since 1929.

Henry Nicholls century leaves New Zealand firmly in driver's seat at stumps on Day 3ENG vs NZ, 2nd Test Day 3: Henry Nicholls' unbeaten century and Rachin Ravindra's 76 powered New Zealand to a 352-run lead over England at The Oval, leaving the visitors firmly in control of the second Test.

Cristiano Ronaldo criticism is just noise: Ruben Dias after Henry remarksFIFA World Cup 2026: Ruben Dias defended Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's draw with DR Congo brought fresh criticism. The Portugal defender said the squad is ignoring outside noise and focusing on Uzbekistan.

Neeraj Chopra breaches CWG mark, his prophecy on Pathirage comes true in Doha DLNeeraj Chopra finished fourth on his return to competition in Doha with a best throw of 85.69m. Sri Lanka's Rumesh Pathirage won with 88.68m, continuing his remarkable run of form in 2026.

SCO 0-1 MOR Highlights: Saibari strike sinks Scotland in BostonScotland (SCO) vs Morocco (MOR), FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: (SCO 0-1 MOR) Morocco backed up their draw against Brazil with a disciplined and deserved victory that puts one foot in the knockout stages. Scotland battled hard after a difficult first half, but now face a daunting final group clash against Brazil needing a result.

Rishabh Pant's return to DC takes shape: All the inside details explainedRishabh Pant is set for a sensational return to Delhi Capitals after the franchise reached an agreement in principle with LSG. The proposed trade involving Kuldeep Yadav now awaits IPL approval.

Diamond League Highlights: Neeraj finishes fourth on return, Pathirage winsDoha Diamond League 2026, Men's javelin Highlights: Neeraj Chopra finished fourth in his first competition of the season, managing a best throw of 85.69m at the Doha Diamond League. Sri Lanka's Rumesh Pathirage claimed victory with a world-leading 88.68m effort.

Ex-India coach urges India Women to fix powerplay bowling issuesWomen's T20 World Cup: Former India head coach WV Raman said that India need to work on their bowling in the powerplay going into their match against Laura Wolvaardt's South Africa on June 21.

Morocco captain and PSG star Achraf Hakimi to face rape trialA French appeals court has upheld the decision to send PSG and Morocco defender Achraf Hakimi to trial over a rape allegation dating back to 2023. Hakimi denies wrongdoing.

Table-tennis legend Sharath Kamal breaks silence on Manika Batra's Asian Games snubSharath Kamal has responded to the controversy over Manika Batra's exclusion from India's Asian Games squad, saying the country's best players must be selected while leaving the door open for a solution.

Nitish Reddy in, Arshdeep Singh out? Ryan ten Doeschate hints at changesIND vs AFG, 3rd ODI: India are set to make a few changes for the third ODI against Afghanistan, with Nitish Reddy available for selection and Arshdeep Singh likely to be rested as the hosts chase a series whitewash in Chennai.

Not Sooryavanshi: Bihar finds another prodigy, 15-year-old hits triple century15-year-old Akshara Gupta delivered a stunning performance in the Bihar Women's Under-19 One Day Trophy, scoring an unbeaten 306 off just 126 balls. With Vaibhav Sooryavanshi bringing attention to Bihar cricket, Akshara's rise further strengthens the state's reputation as a hub of young cricketing talent.

Delhi Capitals to appoint Yuvraj Singh as batting coach after poor IPL 2026: ReportFormer India all-rounder Yuvraj Singh is set to join Delhi Capitals as their batting coach for the IPL 2027 season, marking his first coaching role in the league. The move is part of a major support staff overhaul, with Sourav Ganguly expected to take over as Director of Cricket.

'Hunger never dies': Virat Kohli to play for RCB till 2030, says franchise CEORoyal Challengers Bengaluru CEO Rajesh Menon believes Virat Kohli can continue playing until at least 2030, saying the veteran batter's hunger, fitness and passion for success remain undiminished.

Virat, Rohit's 2027 World Cup future not meant for public consumption: BCCI SecretaryBCCI secretary Saikia refused to discuss Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli's place in India's 2027 World Cup plans, saying such decisions belong in the boardroom. He also said India's bilateral cricket with Bangladesh will be guided strictly by Government of India policy, not political changes there.

Iran to lodge complaint with FIFA over World Cup travel restrictionsFIFA World Cup 2026: Iran will complain to FIFA after saying US travel rules disrupted its World Cup preparation. The restrictions have turned a football campaign into a dispute over access, recovery and competitive fairness.

Neeraj Chopra joins anti-doping fight, launches clean sport campaign with IOAOlympic champion Neeraj Chopra has joined hands with the Indian Olympic Association (IOA) to launch the Clean Sports Campaign, an initiative aimed at educating athletes about the dangers of doping and promoting fair play.

Harry Kane receives iconic gift from Ronaldo Nazario, poses with signed Brazil jerseyFIFA World Cup 2026: Harry Kane was surprised with a signed Brazil shirt from Ronaldo during post-match media duties. The exchange showed how elite forwards remain tied to the idols who shaped their ambitions.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
